checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is gas cap enforced for ALL transaction types (legacy, EIP-2930, EIP-1559, EIP-4844)?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit oversized transaction type 0 (legacy) - verify rejection. 2. Submit oversized transaction type 1 (EIP-2930) - verify rejection. 3. Submit oversized transaction type 2 (EIP-1559) - verify rejection. 4. Submit oversized transaction type 3 (EIP-4844 blob) - verify rejection. 5. For each type, test at exactly gasLimit = 16777217.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0010, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-003, Invariant: INV-010 (SG-003, EIP-7825)

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is multiplication_complexity >= 16 for all ModExp inputs?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Invoke ModExp with base_length=0, modulus_length=0. 2. Invoke ModExp with base_length=1, modulus_length=1. 3. Invoke ModExp with base_length=32, modulus_length=1. 4. For each, verify multiplication_complexity >= 16. 5. Try to find any input combination producing complexity < 16.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0011, Trust Boundary: tb-005, Invariant: INV-001 (SG-001, EIP-7883)

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is multiplication_complexity exactly 16 when max(base_length, modulus_length) <= 32?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Test with base_length=32, modulus_length=32 - expect complexity = 16. 2. Test with base_length=1, modulus_length=32 - expect complexity = 16. 3. Test with base_length=32, modulus_length=1 - expect complexity = 16. 4. Test boundary: base_length=33, modulus_length=32 - expect complexity > 16. 5. Verify exact value of 16 for all small-operand cases.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0012, Trust Boundary: tb-005, Invariant: INV-002 (SG-001, EIP-7883)

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is multiplication_complexity = 2 * ceil(max_length/8)^2 for large operands?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Test max_length=33: expected = 2 * ceil(33/8)^2 = 2 * 5^2 = 50. 2. Test max_length=40: expected = 2 * ceil(40/8)^2 = 2 * 5^2 = 50. 3. Test max_length=41: expected = 2 * ceil(41/8)^2 = 2 * 6^2 = 72. 4. Verify ceiling division (not floor). 5. Test non-multiples of 8: 33, 34, 39, 41, 47, 49.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0013, Trust Boundary: tb-005, Invariant: INV-003 (SG-001, EIP-7883)

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is iteration_count >= 1 for all non-trivial ModExp inputs?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Invoke ModExp with exponent=0, exponent_length=1. 2. Invoke ModExp with exponent=0, exponent_length=32. 3. Verify iteration_count = max(calculated_value, 1) = 1. 4. Test edge cases that might produce iteration_count = 0 before the max() clamp. 5. Confirm no zero-cost execution path exists.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0014, Trust Boundary: tb-005, Invariant: INV-001 (SG-002, EIP-7883)

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is iteration_count exactly 1 for zero exponent with length <= 32?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Invoke ModExp with exponent=0, exponent_length=1. 2. Verify iteration_count = 1 (not 0, not > 1). 3. Test with exponent=0, exponent_length=32. 4. Verify iteration_count = 1. 5. Compare gas costs to ensure correct pricing for zero exponent.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0015, Trust Boundary: tb-005, Invariant: INV-002 (SG-002, EIP-7883)

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is large exponent multiplier 16 (not 8) in iteration_count formula?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Test with exponent_length=33, top_32_bytes with bit_length=1. 2. Expected: iteration_count = 16 * (33-32) + 1 = 17. 3. Old formula would give: 8 * (33-32) + 1 = 9. 4. Verify actual gas cost matches EIP-7883 (multiplier 16). 5. Test across multiple clients for consistency.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0016, Trust Boundary: tb-005, Invariant: INV-003 (SG-002, EIP-7883)

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ModExp gas cost >= 500 for all inputs?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Invoke ModExp with minimal inputs (base_length=1, modulus_length=1, exponent_length=1, exponent=1). 2. Verify gas cost = max(500, computed_cost). 3. Test old minimum of 200 is NOT used. 4. Try various small-input combinations to find minimum. 5. Confirm 500 is the absolute floor.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0017, Trust Boundaries: tb-005, tb-006, Invariant: INV-001 (SG-003, EIP-7883)

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does ModExp gas formula NOT include /3 divisor?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Compute gas for a known input using EIP-7883 formula. 2. Compute gas using EIP-2565 formula (with /3). 3. Invoke ModExp with that input and measure actual gas. 4. Verify actual matches EIP-7883 (no /3), NOT EIP-2565. 5. Expected: EIP-7883 gas ~= 3 * EIP-2565 gas (roughly).
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0018, Trust Boundary: tb-006, Invariant: INV-002 (SG-003, EIP-7883)

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ModExp gas computation deterministic across all clients?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create a test vector of 100+ diverse ModExp inputs. 2. Execute each on geth, nethermind, besu, erigon. 3. Compare gas costs for identical inputs across clients. 4. Any divergence indicates potential consensus bug. 5. Focus on edge cases: boundary values, non-multiples of 8, large exponents.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0019, Trust Boundary: tb-006, Consensus-critical

checklist_item.title: Verify Pre-Condition: Does tx.gasLimit > 16777216 trigger rejection path?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit transaction with gasLimit = 16777217. 2. Trace code execution to verify rejection branch is taken. 3. Confirm transaction is not added to txpool. 4. Verify appropriate error is returned. 5. Test state machine transition: q_check -> q_reject.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0020, Trust Boundary: tb-001, State transition: q_check -> q_reject (SG-001)

checklist_item.title: Verify Pre-Condition: Does tx.gasLimit <= 16777216 trigger acceptance path?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit transaction with gasLimit = 16777216 (exactly at cap). 2. Verify transaction is accepted into txpool. 3. Submit transaction with gasLimit = 16777215. 4. Verify transaction is accepted. 5. Test state machine transition: q_check -> q_accept.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0021, Trust Boundary: tb-001, State transition: q_check -> q_accept (SG-001)

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does block validation catch txpool-bypassing oversized transactions?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Simulate private transaction channel bypassing txpool. 2. Construct block with oversized transaction. 3. Submit block via P2P to honest validator. 4. Verify block is rejected at block validation layer. 5. Test with MEV-Boost simulation if applicable.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0022, Trust Boundary: tb-008, Defense-in-depth validation

checklist_item.title: Verify Boundary Condition: Does txpool use strict comparison (> not >=) for gas cap?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit tx with gasLimit = 16777215 - expect accept. 2. Submit tx with gasLimit = 16777216 - expect accept. 3. Submit tx with gasLimit = 16777217 - expect reject. 4. Audit source code for comparison operator. 5. Confirm >(gasLimit, cap) not >=(gasLimit, cap).
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0023, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Boundary condition at SG-001

checklist_item.title: Verify Boundary Condition: Does block validation use identical comparison to txpool?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Audit block validation code for comparison operator. 2. Confirm identical to txpool (> not >=). 3. Test block with tx gasLimit = 16777216 - should be valid. 4. Test block with tx gasLimit = 16777217 - should be invalid. 5. Cross-check both layers for consistency.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0024, Trust Boundary: tb-003, Boundary condition at SG-002

checklist_item.title: Verify Post-Condition: Is multiplication_complexity = 16 when max_length <= 32?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Trace state transition q_max -> q_small for max_length=32. 2. Verify pre-condition: max_length <= 32. 3. Verify post-condition: complexity == 16. 4. Test boundary: max_length=32 (small path) vs max_length=33 (large path). 5. Confirm correct branch selection.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0025, Trust Boundary: tb-005, State: q_max -> q_small (SG-001, EIP-7883)

checklist_item.title: Verify Post-Condition: Is multiplication_complexity = 2*ceil(max_length/8)^2 when max_length > 32?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Trace state transition q_max -> q_large for max_length=33. 2. Verify pre-condition: max_length > 32. 3. Verify post-condition: complexity = 2 * ceil(33/8)^2 = 50. 4. Test with max_length values 33, 64, 128, 256. 5. Verify formula matches specification exactly.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0026, Trust Boundary: tb-005, State: q_max -> q_large (SG-001, EIP-7883)

checklist_item.title: Verify Post-Condition: Is iteration_count using multiplier 16 for exponent_length > 32?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Trace q_large -> q_compute_large transition for exponent_length=64. 2. Verify formula: 16 * (64-32) + bit_length(top_32). 3. Compare with old formula: 8 * (64-32) + bit_length. 4. Confirm 2x difference in iteration_count. 5. Verify across multiple client implementations.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0027, Trust Boundary: tb-005, State: q_large -> q_compute_large (SG-002, EIP-7883)

checklist_item.title: Is rate-limiting effective against cap-sized transaction flooding?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit many transactions with gasLimit = 16777216 from single sender. 2. Verify per-sender limits are enforced. 3. Measure node performance under transaction flood. 4. Verify EIP-1559 base fee provides economic rate limiting. 5. Test txpool eviction policies for cap-sized transactions.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0028, Trust Boundary: tb-001, STRIDE-Spoofing-tb-001

checklist_item.title: Verify Defense-in-Depth: Does block validation catch compromised txpool leaks?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Simulate compromised txpool allowing oversized transactions. 2. Block builder includes such transaction. 3. Submit resulting block to honest validators. 4. Verify all honest validators reject the block. 5. Confirm defense-in-depth model is functional.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0029, Trust Boundaries: tb-002, tb-003, STRIDE-Tampering-tb-002

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Do all honest validators reject malicious builder blocks?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Construct block with malicious builder containing oversized tx. 2. Broadcast to network of honest validators. 3. Verify 100% rejection rate. 4. Test on diverse client implementations. 5. Monitor for any validator accepting the invalid block.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0030, Trust Boundary: tb-003, STRIDE-Tampering-tb-003

checklist_item.title: Is number of cap-sized transactions per block bounded by block_gas_limit / 16777216?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Calculate floor(30000000 / 16777216) = 1 for typical 30M block limit. 2. Attempt to include 2 cap-sized transactions in one block. 3. Verify block is rejected due to exceeding block gas limit. 4. Confirm natural bound is enforced. 5. This is by-design behavior, not a vulnerability.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0031, Trust Boundary: tb-003, STRIDE-DoS-tb-003

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ModExp charged >= 500 gas for all edge case inputs?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Invoke ModExp with base_length=0, modulus_length=0, exponent_length=0. 2. Invoke ModExp with base_length=0, modulus_length=0, exponent_length=1, exponent=0. 3. Invoke ModExp with maximum possible input lengths. 4. Verify all cases charge at least 500 gas. 5. Look for any input bypassing the minimum floor.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0033, Trust Boundary: tb-005, STRIDE-Tampering-tb-005

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is gas cost proportional to actual computation for ModExp?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Benchmark ModExp execution times for various inputs. 2. Compare wall-clock time to gas charged. 3. Look for inputs where time/gas ratio is unusually high. 4. Test inputs known to be expensive (large exponents). 5. Verify no cheap-but-slow computation paths exist.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0034, Trust Boundary: tb-005, STRIDE-DoS-tb-005, Core EIP-7883 security goal

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ModExp gas identical across all client implementations?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create comprehensive ModExp test vector suite. 2. Execute on geth, nethermind, besu, erigon, reth. 3. Compare gas values for each test case. 4. Focus on boundary values and edge cases. 5. Any divergence = consensus-critical bug.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0035, Trust Boundary: tb-006, STRIDE-Tampering-tb-006
